message:
🌱 distribution: Registry - Allow minimum TLS version to be configurable (#796)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "distribution-2807-registry-allow-minimum-tls-version-to-be-configurable",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "distribution: Registry - Allow minimum TLS version to be configurable",
+    "description": "Registry - Allow minimum TLS version to be configurable. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current distribution deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your distribution version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution\nhelm list -n distribution 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working distribution installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review distribution configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant distribution configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution\nkubectl get configmap -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=distribution\n```\nSimilar to #2715 I have a high-security environment where no TLS1.0 or TLS1.1 traffic is allowed, and registry currently hardcodes the minimum TLS version."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Registry - Allow minimum TLS version to be configurable",
+        "description": "```\nhttp:\n  tls:\n    minimumtls: tls1.2\n```\nAn omitted or unrecognized string results in the same TLS1.0 support we have today, and allowed strings are in the updated docs - \"tls1.0\", \"tls1.1\", \"tls1.2\".\n```yaml\nhttp:\n  tls:\n    minimumtls: tls1.2\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution\nkubectl get events -n distribution --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Registry - Allow minimum TLS version to be configurable\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "```\nhttp:\n  tls:\n    minimumtls: tls1.2\n```\nAn omitted or unrecognized string results in the same TLS1.0 support we have today, and allowed strings are in the updated docs - \"tls1.0\", \"tls1.1\", \"tls1.2\".",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "http:\n  tls:\n    minimumtls: tls1.2",
+        "http:\n  tls:\n    minimumtls: tls1.2",
+        "time=\"2020-06-26T18:48:58Z\" level=warning msg=\"Ignoring unrecognized environment variable REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_MINIMUMTLS\""
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "distribution",
+      "sandbox",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "distribution"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/distribution/distribution/issues/2807",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/distribution/distribution",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/distribution/distribution/pull/2808"
+    },
+    "reactions": 1,
+    "comments": 19,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "docker",
+      "curl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with distribution installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:32:09.860Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
